question: I speak without a mouth, answer without ears, and disappear the moment I am named. What am I?
The more you take from me, the larger I become. What am I?
I can fill a room but take up no space. What am I?
I have cities but no houses, forests but no trees, and rivers but no water. What am I?
I am always in front of you, but I can never be seen. What am I?
What has roots nobody sees, is taller than trees, up it goes, yet never grows?
I shrink smaller every time I take a bath. What am I?
I have a neck but no head, arms but no hands, and I can hold you. What am I?
I am not alive, but I grow. I do not have lungs, but I need air. What am I?
I have keys but open no locks, space but no room, and you can enter but not go in. What am I?
I am taken from a mine and shut in a wooden case, but I am used by almost everyone. What am I?
What comes once in a minute, twice in a moment, but never in a thousand years?
I run all day but never get tired, and I have no legs. What am I?
I can be cracked, made, told, and played. What am I?
The person who makes me does not need me. The person who buys me does not use me. The person who uses me does not know it. What am I?
I am full of holes, but I can still hold water. What am I?
What has many teeth but cannot bite?
What gets wetter the more it dries?
I go up but never come down. What am I?
I have one eye but cannot see. What am I?
What has hands but cannot clap?
I am light as a feather, yet the strongest person cannot hold me for long. What am I?
I have branches but no fruit, trunk, or leaves. What am I?
What can travel around the world while staying in the same corner?
I have words but never speak. What am I?
What begins with T, ends with T, and has T inside?
I am bought by the yard but worn by the foot. What am I?
What has an end but no beginning, a home but no family, and a space without a room?
I am seen in the water, but I never get wet. What am I?
I can be broken without being touched. What am I?
I am always hungry and must always be fed, but if you give me water, I die. What am I?
What has a head, a tail, is brown, and has no legs?
I am easy to lift but hard to throw. What am I?
What kind of room has no doors or windows?
What has a ring but no finger?
I start out tall, but the longer I stand, the shorter I grow. What am I?
What has four legs in the morning, two at noon, and three in the evening?
I move faster when I am still and slower when I am chased. What am I?
What building has the most stories?
I am a word of letters three; add two and fewer there will be. What am I?
What is always coming but never arrives?
I can be long or short, grown or bought, painted or bare, round or square. What am I?
What can you catch but not throw?
I have no life, but I can die. What am I?
I am a box that holds keys without locks, yet they can unlock your soul. What am I?
What is so fragile that saying its name breaks it?
What can run but cannot walk, has a mouth but cannot talk, has a bed but cannot sleep?
I am yours, but other people use me more than you do. What am I?
What has no beginning, end, or middle?
I can be written, spoken, exposed, or kept, but never held in your hand. What am I?
What disappears as soon as you say its name?
I am found in seconds, minutes, and centuries, but not in days, weeks, or years. What am I?
I point in every direction, but I never move. What am I?
What is cut on a table but never eaten?
I am always running but never move from my place. What am I?
What gets sharper the more you use it?
I have a spine but no bones. What am I?
What is black when clean and white when dirty?
I can bring tears to your eyes, resurrect the dead, make you smile, and reverse time. What am I?
What belongs to you but is used by everyone around you?
